title: Release Version Package Drift
description: Find new inconsistencies among package.json versions, workspace manifests, changelog, release docs, rc gate scripts, package README files, publish/release tooling, and current branch state. Skip duplicates already in issues.jsonl. Evidence must be file:line.
findings: Found 3 new release/package drift issues: current 2.2.0-rc.5 manifests lack matching changelog/status, shared package is omitted from npm verification, and tag-version tooling rewrites quarantined private package versions.
issues_found: [{"title":"Current 2.2.0-rc.5 manifests have no matching release notes or README status","severity":"medium","description":"Root and publishable package manifests are at 2.2.0-rc.5, but the top changelog entry is still 2.0.1 GA and README status still says v2.0.0 GA; even the changelog comparison link remains anchored at v2.0.0-rc.4, so a 2.2.0-rc.5 release/tag would ship without matching public release notes or status guidance. Evidence: package.json:4, CHANGELOG.md:8, CHANGELOG.md:1469, README.md:317.","location":"package.json:4","fix_direction":"Add a 2.2.0-rc.5 changelog entry or Unreleased section with correct compare links, and update README status to the current intended release line before tagging.","affected_components":["release docs","CHANGELOG.md","README.md","package manifests"]},{"title":"Release checklist omits @fenglimg/fabric-shared from post-publish npm verification","severity":"medium","description":"RELEASING says to verify release packages are visible on npm, but the checklist only runs npm view for @fenglimg/fabric-cli and @fenglimg/fabric-server. @fenglimg/fabric-shared is a versioned published package with files configured, so a failed or stale shared publish would not be caught by the documented gate. Evidence: RELEASING.md:48, RELEASING.md:51, RELEASING.md:52, packages/shared/package.json:2, packages/shared/package.json:3, packages/shared/package.json:67.","location":"RELEASING.md:51","fix_direction":"Add npm view @fenglimg/fabric-shared version to the post-publish checklist and keep the package list generated from the same publishable-workspace source as the version sync gate.","affected_components":["RELEASING.md","@fenglimg/fabric-shared","release verification"]},{"title":"apply-tag-version rewrites quarantined private package versions unlike sync-versions","severity":"low","description":"sync-versions explicitly exempts private packages such as the quarantined HTTP package from the release version line, but apply-tag-version collects every packages/* directory and rewrites every manifest version. That means a tag publish mutates @fenglimg/fabric-server-http-experimental from 0.0.0-quarantine to the public tag in CI logs/working tree, contradicting the quarantine marker and the validator's own rule. Evidence: scripts/sync-versions.mjs:62, scripts/sync-versions.mjs:64, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:40, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:50, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:53, packages/server-http-experimental/package.json:3, packages/server-http-experimental/package.json:4.","location":"scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:50","fix_direction":"Make apply-tag-version share the same manifest collection/filter as sync-versions, skipping private packages or at least preserving 0.0.0-quarantine for the quarantined package.","affected_components":["scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs","scripts/sync-versions.mjs","packages/server-http-experimental","release tooling"]}]

title: Generated Managed Artifact Drift
description: Find new drift between canonical templates/source and checked-in generated artifacts for .claude/.codex/.cursor, managed blocks, hook configs, skill copies, snapshots, and installer expectations. Skip duplicates already registered such as Cursor bootstrap stale, duplicate hook copies, archive-hint naming unless you find materially new evidence. Evidence must be file:line.
findings: Found 3 new managed-artifact drift issues: root AGENTS managed block stale, Codex/Cursor hook config order drift, and docs still saying Cursor hooks are pending.
issues_found: [{"title":"Root AGENTS managed bootstrap block is stale relative to BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL","severity":"medium","description":"The checked-in root AGENTS.md managed block begins with a shorter store-only text at AGENTS.md:13, but the canonical installer source starts different byte-locked content at packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts:63, and install tests require the AGENTS.md managed body to byte-equal the .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot/BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL at packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:679 and :764. This means the active Codex policy file in the repo is not what a fresh install would generate.","location":"AGENTS.md:13; packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts:63; packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:764","fix_direction":"Regenerate the root AGENTS.md managed block from BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL/.fabric/AGENTS.md and add a repo-level drift check that compares checked-in AGENTS.md managed body against the canonical snapshot.","affected_components":["AGENTS.md","packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts","bootstrap propagation","Codex managed block"]},{"title":"Checked-in Codex and Cursor hook configs reverse the template PreToolUse hook order","severity":"low","description":"The hook config templates install knowledge-hint-narrow before cite-policy-evict for Codex and Cursor (packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json:13, packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json:10), but the checked-in .codex/hooks.json and .cursor/hooks.json put cite-policy-evict first (.codex/hooks.json:14, .cursor/hooks.json:14). Installer code merges from those templates, so the dogfood configs no longer represent a fresh install.","location":".codex/hooks.json:14; .cursor/hooks.json:14; packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json:13; packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json:10","fix_direction":"Regenerate .codex/hooks.json and .cursor/hooks.json from the hook config templates, or make the intended order explicit in templates/tests and add a drift assertion for checked-in client configs.","affected_components":[".codex/hooks.json",".cursor/hooks.json","packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs","PreToolUse hook config"]},{"title":"Docs still describe Cursor hook support as pending even though installer and checked-in configs install Cursor hooks","severity":"medium","description":"docs/initialization.md still says Phase 4 writes only .claude/.codex hooks and that Cursor has no Stop-hook surface as of 2026-05 (docs/initialization.md:25 and :222), and docs/roadmap.md still tracks Cursor Stop-hook support as pending (docs/roadmap.md:113). Current installer destinations include .cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs at packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:186, and the checked-in Cursor config wires stop/sessionStart/preToolUse hooks at .cursor/hooks.json:4.","location":"docs/initialization.md:25; docs/initialization.md:222; docs/roadmap.md:113; packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:186; .cursor/hooks.json:4","fix_direction":"Update initialization/roadmap docs to state Cursor hooks are currently installed, including stop/sessionStart/preToolUse/postToolUse/sessionEnd as appropriate; move the old pending note to explicit historical migration context if needed.","affected_components":["docs/initialization.md","docs/roadmap.md","Cursor hook install docs","packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts"]}]

title: Tests Snapshots Runtime Drift
description: Find tests/snapshots that validate old behavior, omit current public surfaces, or contradict runtime implementation. Focus on snapshots, imports of retired modules, comments/assertions that make old behavior authoritative, and tests that falsely pass because they validate stale fixtures. Skip existing issues. Evidence must be file:line.
findings: Found 2 new low-severity shared test-seed/runtime drift issues; skipped existing command/server/bootstrap drift duplicates already registered as ISS-20260608-001..049.
issues_found: [{"title":"Shared test seed reverses MCP payload guard boundary semantics","severity":"low","description":"docs/test-seed/shared.md says payloads exactly at the warn/hard thresholds belong to the warn/throw side (>=16K warn / >=64K throw), but the runtime uses strict greater-than checks and tests assert 16384 has no warning and 65536 does not throw; the integration test also has a stale summary comment that says the opposite of its own cases.","location":"docs/test-seed/shared.md:49","fix_direction":"Update shared.md I4/T3 and the stale boundary-test summary comment to state the current contract: bytes > warn emits warning, bytes > hard throws; equality stays on the safe side except 65536 still returns a warning because it is > warn.","affected_components":["docs/test-seed/shared.md","packages/shared/src/node/mcp-payload-guard.ts","packages/shared/test/mcp-payload-guard.test.ts","packages/shared/test/integration/mcp-payload-guard-boundary.test.ts"]},{"title":"Shared test seed still advertises retired bootstrap-guide export and rule-test-index schema","severity":"low","description":"docs/test-seed/shared.md lists ./node/bootstrap-guide and packages/shared/src/node/bootstrap-guide.ts as current public surface and names rule-test-index in the schema set, but the actual package exports ./templates/bootstrap-canonical, the old bootstrap-guide file is absent, and current tests import knowledge-test-index/bootstrap-canonical instead.","location":"docs/test-seed/shared.md:16","fix_direction":"Refresh docs/test-seed/shared.md from packages/shared/package.json and current tests: replace bootstrap-guide with ./templates/bootstrap-canonical, replace rule-test-index with knowledge-test-index, and update source traceability paths.","affected_components":["docs/test-seed/shared.md","packages/shared/package.json","packages/shared/test/knowledge-test-index.test.ts","packages/shared/test/templates/bootstrap-canonical.test.ts"]}]

title: Config Schema Doc I18n Drift
description: Find new mismatches between fabric.config schema/defaults, config docs, i18n strings, CLI help/output, package docs, and runtime command behavior. Skip existing issues about serve strings, force-only flags, source naming, active_project unless materially new. Evidence must be file:line.
findings: Found one new config-schema drift: the checked-in JSON Schema still models a retired config surface and rejects current keys used by the repo/runtime.
issues_found: [{"title":"Checked-in fabric-config JSON Schema still describes retired config surface","severity":"medium","description":"`schemas/fabric-config.json` is stale: it sets `additionalProperties:false` but only allows old top-level keys, so the repo's current root `fabric.config.json` with `embed_enabled`/`embed_model` is schema-invalid, while the live TypeScript schema defines those fields. The same JSON Schema still allows retired v1 clientPaths (`windsurf`, `rooCode`, `geminiCLI`) and `externalFixturePath`, even though the TS schema says those client keys are rejected and dev fixture path now comes only from `EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH`; docs also still list `externalFixturePath` as optional.","location":"schemas/fabric-config.json:7; fabric.config.json:2; packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts:7; packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts:441; packages/cli/src/dev-mode.ts:30; docs/configuration.md:282","fix_direction":"Regenerate or remove `schemas/fabric-config.json` from the live `fabricConfigSchema`, including current fields such as embed/retrieval/store config, deleting retired v1 clients and `externalFixturePath`; also remove the stale `externalFixturePath` section from docs or mark it historical and point users to `EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH`.","affected_components":["schemas/fabric-config.json","fabric.config.json","packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts","packages/cli/src/dev-mode.ts","docs/configuration.md","config editor validation"]}]

title: Knowledge Store Migration Tail Drift
description: Find new tail-end migration contradictions around .fabric/knowledge versus mounted stores, agents.meta derived state, event/metric ledgers, stable_id, pending/review/archive/import flows, docs, skill templates, and server tools. Skip existing issues about store-backed pending glob, counters docs, fab_recall-first lint unless materially new. Evidence must be file:line.
findings: Found 2 new medium tail-drift issues: active docs still teach project-local .fabric/knowledge as source of truth, and --fix-knowledge still advertises demote/archive git-mv repairs that store cutover currently disables.
issues_found: [{"title":"User and schema docs still teach project-local .fabric/knowledge as the knowledge source after store-only cutover","severity":"medium","description":"docs/USER-QUICKSTART.md:10 says team knowledge is stored under .fabric/knowledge, :20 tells developers to author .fabric/knowledge/<type>, and :60 shows pending there; docs/data-schema.md:15-16 and :26 define the same repo/home roots, while AGENTS.md:5 and :19 now require mounted stores only.","location":"docs/USER-QUICKSTART.md:10","fix_direction":"Rewrite onboarding and data-schema storage sections around mounted stores/current write store paths, remove direct authoring of project-local .fabric/knowledge, and mark any old dual-root examples as legacy migration material.","affected_components":["docs/USER-QUICKSTART.md","docs/data-schema.md","docs/SPEC_INTERNAL.md","knowledge store migration docs"]},{"title":"doctor --fix-knowledge advertises demote/archive git-mv repairs that store cutover currently no-ops","severity":"medium","description":"packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:93-97 and :1577 still advertise demote/archive/frontmatter+git mv fixes and docs/configuration.md:289-291 says --fix-knowledge rewrites maturity and relocates stale entries, but packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts:1834-1838 says store-aware versions must be implemented before re-enabling and :657-670 returns empty candidates for those inspections.","location":"packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:93","fix_direction":"Either re-enable store-aware demote/stale/pending archive inspections over ~/.fabric/stores/*/knowledge, or narrow CLI help/docs/previews to the repair actions that actually still run after the store cutover.","affected_components":["packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts","packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts","docs/configuration.md","docs/getting-started.md","doctor --fix-knowledge"]}]
